symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive exertional dyspnoea, now breathless at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Effort intolerance progressing to rest dyspnoea reflects rising pulmonary venous pressure.
Positional and nocturnal dyspnoea are relatively specific for raised left-heart filling pressures.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: COPD or lower respiratory tract infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marked orthopnoea/PND is not typical of a primary airways problem.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral symmetrical pitting leg swelling up to the knees

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical dependent oedema reflects systemic venous congestion.
DVT swelling is characteristically unilateral; bilateral symmetry points away from it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid weight gain ~4 kg over one week (patient-obtainable, home scales)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid weight gain is a quantitative marker of fluid retention/decompensation; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth, unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe generalised fatigue and weakness, now chair-bound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low cardiac output causes fatigue but is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated jugular venous pressure (~5 cm above sternal angle)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective systemic venous congestion — sealed clinician sign, not obtainable over chat.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: COPD or lower respiratory tract infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised JVP points to cardiac congestion rather than a primary airways cause.
An S3 is relatively specific for a volume-loaded, failing ventricle — sealed clinician sign.
Basal crackles reflect pulmonary congestion — sealed clinician sign; non-specific alone but corroborative in context.
Displaced apex suggests a dilated ventricle/cardiomegaly — sealed clinician sign.
Hypoxia and tachypnoea from pulmonary congestion — clinician-measured here; not reliably obtainable without a validated home device.

symptoms.symptom_name: Echocardiographic structural abnormality (impaired systolic function / dilated ventricle)

investigation_details.expected_result: Impaired left ventricular systolic function / dilated cardiomyopathy with reduced ejection fraction
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The structural cause and ejection fraction remain unconfirmed, and guideline-directed therapy cannot be optimised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Echocardiography confirms the underlying structural diagnosis — never available in real time on telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, purulent cough and wheeze

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: COPD or lower respiratory tract infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No infective or bronchospastic features reduces the likelihood of a primary respiratory cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Gradual congestive onset over weeks rather than sudden pleuritic breathlessness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden pleuritic pain with acute desaturation would suggest PE; a gradual congestive course argues against it, though PE is not fully excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: On bisoprolol, low-dose ramipril and a prescribed loop diuretic; no extra diuretic taken

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Established cardiac disease on guideline-directed therapy; establishes baseline and helps assess adherence-related precipitants.
Absence of chest pain lowers but does not exclude an ischaemic precipitant; a silent/atypical ACS still needs ECG and troponin.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together, rest dyspnoea + orthopnoea/PND + symmetrical oedema + rapid weight gain + raised JVP + S3 + basal crackles form a coherent decompensated-heart-failure picture that is far stronger than any single feature.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identify an ischaemic precipitant or arrhythmia driving decompensation — requires in-person equipment.
Screen for an acute coronary precipitant even without chest pain.
Confirm pulmonary oedema/effusions and exclude an infective infiltrate.
Baseline before and during loop-diuretic decongestion; guards against acute kidney injury and electrolyte disturbance.
Supports the diagnosis of decompensated heart failure and gauges severity.
Confirms the underlying structural/cardiomyopathic cause and ejection fraction to direct therapy.
